response: Data collection begins with defining the study objectives and ensuring data sources are reliable and relevant. Data from existing records is compared with data gathered through direct observation to ensure consistency. Data analysis techniques are applied to data sets to identify patterns and potential risk factors. Data must be cleaned to remove inconsistencies before data visualization tools can be effectively used. Data is categorized based on variables to ensure data integrity during statistical evaluation. Data from different regions is cross-referenced with data from similar studies to validate findings. Data interpretation requires careful review of data trends to avoid misleading conclusions. Data is documented thoroughly to ensure data accessibility for future research and verification.
